The notification provider to be enabled is included in the documentation. 

For example, process-specific notifications are provided for the process Verify Journal Entries

  • The app documentation help for SAP Fiori app F2547A Verify General Journal Entries - New Version explains the notification provider is JE_VER_NOTIF_PROV and how to configure it.
  • A deeper look at the notification provider class by a developer confirms there are 3 notification types provided:
    • Posting Completed – with text “General journal entry <journal entry number> has been posted.”
    • Posting Rejected - with text “General journal entry <journal entry number> has been rejected.”
    • Posting Error - with text “Fail to post general journal entry <journal entry number>.”

An example of how the Posting Completed notification appears to users is shown below: 

Selecting the notification navigates to the related app – which in this case is SAP Fiori app F0717 Manage Journal Entries to show the posted journal entry.

Refer to SAP Help – SAP S/4HANA - Apps for General Ledger Accounting - Postings and Journal Entries - Verify General Journal Entries - Enabling and Extending Workflows - Customizing for Configuration - Expert - Back End Customizing

 

Becoming a SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA guru

You’ll find much more on our  SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA topic page

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA RIG and Customer Care team.

entry.title: Join the SAP S/4HANA Service, Private Cloud Edition Early Adopter Care Program
entry.content:
Are you running classic Customer Service (CS) within the Compatibility Scope in S/4HANA*, and wondering how to transition to SAP S/4HANA Service?

You’re not alone. Many organizations are asking the same questions: Where do we start? What’s the benefit? Who can guide us?

We understand the challenges, and we’re here to support your transformation journey.

* Note: The Compatibility Scope for CS is set to expire at the end of 2030 (unlike most cases, which expire at the end of 2025). This makes it essential to begin planning your transition. For more details, refer to SAP Note 2269324 and this blog post.

Why join this program?

The SAP S/4HANA Service, Private Cloud Edition Early Adopter Care Program is your opportunity to engage directly with SAP’s product development teams. Our mission is simple: to refine and evolve our solutions based on real customer needs. Your voice matters.

As a participant, you’ll gain direct access to SAP product experts who can guide you through your transition from CS to S/4HANA Service. You’ll receive support with product-related questions and benefit from close monitoring of your critical issues. Most importantly, you’ll have the opportunity to share your feedback and help shape the future of SAP Service – at no cost to you.

Who should join?

This program is designed for customers who are licensed for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and are running on SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS0 or higher. If you're still using classic CS in the Compatibility Scope within SAP S/4HANA and have concrete plans for a proof of concept or implementation project, this is your chance to get expert guidance. We’re looking for participants who are ready to engage regularly, share their experiences, and become reference customers.

To make the most of the program, you will need a dedicated project team and a well-defined internal setup to drive the implementation.

How to apply

Apply now via the SAP Influence Portal and become part of a select group helping shape the future of SAP service management capabilities. Let’s build the future of SAP S/4HANA Service together.

Join the SAP S/4HANA Service, Private Cloud Edition Early Adopter Care Program and take the next step in your transformation journey.

If you have any questions, feel free to contact us: SAP_EAC_S4H_Service@sap.com.

Download a copy of the one-pager (image below).

Learn more about SAP S/4HANA Service

  • SAP S/4HANA Service | SAP Community – collection of webinars, blog articles, links to certification program and other materials. It’s a great entry point to see all resources on SAP S/4HANA Service.
  • Recap of SAP S/4HANA 2023 Highlights Webinar: Service – 30-min webinar I conducted. I show some concepts in diagrams and show system demonstrations of highlights in the 2023 release (FPS0) for SAP S/4HANA Service.

@o_frick @Holk_H

entry.title: Part 2 Webinar Entitlement Mgmt: Preview for Scope Item “5SW – Subscription Management for Software"
entry.content:
Join us for this webinar on SAP Entitlement Management, offering Part 2 of the deep dive preview of the planned Scope Item "5SW – Subscription Management for Software" on Tuesday, June 24, 2025, at 4:00 PM CEST / 10:00 AM EST. 

Register now!

This one-hour session is Part 2 of the webinars on scope item 5SW, starting with a short recap of the content of the first webinar held on May 13, 2024, which you can review here,

Part 2 of the webinar will explain and show demos for various subscription change processes which are planned to be supported by the scope item, as well as automated application, tenant and capability decommissioning. 

In addition, we will also have a look into key elements of the system setup for the scope item, for which a detailed setup guide and end to end test script is planned to be made available with the 2508 release. 

We will also give an outlook on the product direction and product vision for "Subscription Management for Software", with SAP Business Data Cloud leveraging license consumption, software usage and adoption data as the basis for customer success, using Analytics Cloud stories, AI Agents, or integration to Gainsight Customer Success. 

Register now and review the detailed agenda below!

More information on EMS - see my Summary Blog on SAP Entitlement Management 

Customer Webinar Part 2 – SAP Entitlement Management:
Deep Dive Preview for Scope Item “5SW – Subscription Management for Software”

Date: Tuesday, June 24, 2025
Time: 4:00 PM Central European Summer Time / 10:00 AM EST
Duration: 1 hour

Agenda:

  • Introduction & Overview of Scope Item 5SW
    • Purpose & business value of Subscription Management for Software
    • Key capabilities: End-to-end integration and automation from sales to fulfillment/provisioning, billing, and revenue recognition
  • Live Demo: Subscription Management in Action – Subscription Change Processes, System Setup for Automated Provisioning, Business Data Cloud and AI Providing Insights for Customer Success Processes
    • Summary of demo flow in first session on May 13, 2025 (review here)
    • Demo of different change processes, e.g.
      • Change of licensed quantity and subscription end date
      • Add-on contract items for more users, more documents, additional test tenants, free trial licenses
      • Contract replacement moving to higher license edition without tenant disruption
      • End of subscription and entitlement license and tenant de-commissioning
    • Key elements of the system setup to achieve automated application, tenant, license metric, and application capability provisioning and de-commissioning
    • SAP Business Data Cloud – leverage license consumption, software usage and adoption data as the basis for customer success, using Analytics Cloud stories, AI Agents, or integration to Gainsight Customer Success
  • Summary, Q&A

entry.title: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Which notification to use when – Notifications for Admins and Developers
entry.content:
TL;DR – Understand the basics behind notifications such as the Notification Channel and the Notification Providers including: what's involved in creating a custom Notification Provider for your own use cases, FAQ, and troubleshooting tips.  These notifications are relevant for Flexible Workflow, classic Workflow, Situation Handling (standard and extended), Smart Business KPI alerts, and process-specific notifications. This blog post is provided for Administrators and Developers. 

A very common business requirement is to notify someone that something has happened and, if possible, get them started on responding to it. 

In this blog post - Part 4 - as an administrator or developer you will learn what's "behind the covers" of notifications. This includes:

  • Configuring the Notification Channel and Notification Hub - how notifications are collected and distributed to users
  • Enabling Notifications in the SAP Fiori launchpad - settings you can adjust if needed
  • Authorizing Users for Notifications - making sure users can trigger and receive notifications
  • Activating Notification Providers - that generate the notifications for different use cases
  • Understanding the Notification Provider class - if you need to dig deeper to confirm provided notification features
  • Creating your own custom Notification Provider - where to find example code
  • Federating notifications to SAP Build Work Zone - including Secure Store and Forward (SSF) and sensitive data options
  • Providing push notifications
  • Echoing notifications to email 
  • FAQ and troubleshooting tips - including how to find notifications at runtime, as in the example below using ABAP Development Tools (ADT in Eclipse)

Finding notification runtime details in ABAP Development Tools in Eclipse

In other blog post of this series you will learn:

  • Part 1 - SAP Fiori notification techniques available in SAP S/4HANA and when to use them; when to use email notifications to complement or supplement SAP Fiori notifications. SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Which notification to use when – Workflow, Situation Handling, KPIs, etc
  • Part 2 - The impact on business users in SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Which notification to use when – Impact on business users   
  • Part 3 - For Key Users, Functional Consultants and UX Architects: Where & how to configure each notification technique in SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Which notification to use when – Where and how to configure

Configuring the Notification Channel and Notification Hub

Behind every notification is a Notification Provider that generates the notifications and provides the notification features. 

Worth knowing: More than 80 notification providers are delivered with SAP S/4HANA.

The Notification Channel collects the notifications from the active Notification Providers into the Notification Channel Hub – from where they are distributed to users.

The Notification Channel is a capability of the ABAP Platform's SAP Gateway Foundation, that uses ABAP Push Channels.  ABAP Push Channels (APC) enable bi-directional communication between the ABAP Platform and Users - even when the user has not initiated a request.  For the notification channel, ABAP Push Channels use the integration with WebSockets.  Refer to

  • SAP Help – ABAP Platform – Gateway Foundation – Notification Channel
  • SAP Help - ABAP Platform - ABAP Development Tools: User Guide - Working with ABAP Push Channels

There is a small amount of once-off configuration that you - as an administrator - complete to activate the Notification Channel and Notification Hub.  This configuration can be found in the Implementation Guide (transaction SPRO) in section ABAP Platform > Notification Channel. Refer to

  • SAP Help – ABAP Platform – Gateway Foundation – Notification Channel Configuration. 

You will need authorizations to complete this configuration - templates are provided for Hub and Channel administrators.  Refer to 

  • SAP Help – ABAP Platform – Gateway Foundation - Notification Channel User Roles and Authorizations

 

The configuration includes mapping and activating the push channel for notifications SAP_WEBSOCKET  in transaction /IWNGW/VNPUSHP.

 

Enabling Notifications in the SAP Fiori launchpad

Notifications are a standard cross-functional feature of the SAP Fiori launchpad and are typically activated by default as part of running the task list SAP_FIORI_FOUNDATION_S4 and selecting the task Configure FLP for Notification (/UI2/FLP_CUS_CONF). 

Hint: For many SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition customers, your ECS team will already have run these for you. You can review which task lists have been run previously in transaction STC02.

If you need to review or adjust the standard settings refer to: SAP Help - SAP Fiori launchpad - Administration guide - Enabling Notifications in the Launchpad

Authorizing Users for Notifications

Before using notifications, your security administrator must authorize users to trigger (produce) and receive (consume) notifications. This is a standard ABAP authorization and you can use the provided Insert Templates options to simplify adding these authorizations to your roles - templates /IWNGW/RT_USER_PRODU and /IWNGW/RT_USER_CONSU respectively.  For example, you can use the templates in Role Maintenance transaction PFCG in the Change Authorizations dialog. Refer to

  • SAP Help – ABAP Platform – Gateway Foundation - Notification Channel User Roles and Authorizations
  • SAP Note 2954378 - The Gateway Error Log shows: No authorization to access service group '/IWNGW/NOTIFICATION'

Important: Because you usually want all users to potentially trigger or receive notifications, you should consider adding these authorizations to a role assigned to every user of the SAP Fiori launchpad (FLP). For example, you might add them to a custom version of the provided every FLP user role Z_FIORI_FOUNDATION_USER.

  • This role is generated when you run task list SAP_FIORI_FOUNDATION_S4 when you set up your SAP Fiori launchpad for SAP S/4HANA system initially.
  • The role generation task can be re-run when you upgrade your SAP S/4HANA system to add new features.  So when using a custom version of the role, make sure you cross-check the generated role for authorizations change you may need to include in your custom every FLP user role.

Testing the Notification Channel

You can run the transaction /IWNGW/BEP_DEMO to test that the notification channel hub is working.  You will need to activate the demo notification provider - i.e. tick a checkbox - before running the program. Refer to SAP Help – ABAP Platform – Gateway Foundation – Testing the Demo Provider

You can choose from 2 types of demo notification – Leave Request or Purchase Order – and which user will receive the notifications. Run the program (select Execute) to generate the selected demo notifications to the selected user.

The program will confirm if a notification has been created and provide the technical runtime id.

The demo notifications will appear in the launchpad of the selected user as per the below screenshot.

 

Understanding notification providers

Notification Providers generate the notifications at runtime. 

Each Notification Provider in SAP S/4HANA is technically an ABAP Class that supports the ABAP Interface /IWNGW/IF_NOTIF_PROVIDER or /IWNGW/IF_NOTIF_PROVIDER_EXT.

Worth knowing: The interface /IWNGW/IF_NOTIF_PROVIDER_EXT provides additional options for notification types, for example where you want to support echoing of notifications to email.

 

 

You can view all the notification providers and their underlying ABAP Classes in transaction /IWNGW/BEP_NPREG.   

To use the notification types, the notification provider must be marked as active in transaction /IWNGW/VB_REG_P.  This is typically performed by an Administrator, alongside the other prerequisites for the notification channel.

Hint: As a minimum you are recommended to activate at least the most commonly used cross-functional notification providers, i.e. 

  • Workflow - provider SAP_BUSINESS_WORKFLOW
  • Situation Handling - providers SIT_NOTIF_PROV, SIT_NOTIF_PROV_EXT, SIT2_NOTIF_PROV
  • Smart Business KPI - provider /SSB/NOTIFICATION_PROVIDER
  • Demo (for verifying the notification channel is working) - provider /IWNGW/DEMO_EXT

 

A notification provider can support multiple notification types.

Each notification type is assigned a unique Notification Type key.

For each Notification Type, the provider manages:

  • Notification Type name shown in the SAP Fiori launchpad – User Actions - Settings – Notifications section where the user sets their notification preferences
  • Subject text of the notification type
  • Long text of the notification type
  • Optionally, parameters that can be included in the text(s)
  • Optionally, quick actions for the notification type
  • Optionally, whether notifications of this notification type can be grouped (type “Is Groupable”)
  • Optionally, whether notifications of this notification type can be echoed to email (type “Is Email Enabled”)
  • Optionally, version id for the notification type

Creating Custom Notification Providers

YES! You can create custom notification providers creating your own notification provider class.

You will need an ABAP Developer to create the notification provider class. 

Your ABAP Developer will need authorizations to create the class as provided by role template /IWNGW/RT_NP_DEV. Refer to:

  • SAP Help – ABAP Platform – Gateway Foundation - Notification Channel User Roles and Authorizations

You will find example code for the class in SAP Help – ABAP Platform – Gateway Foundation – Develop Notification Provider and the example code in Sample Provider.  This includes a Call Notification API you can use to test your provider.

Once your custom notification provider class is available, to generate notifications you will first need to register your custom notification provider class in transaction /IWNGW/BEP_NPREG, and activate your custom notification provider in transaction /IWNGW/VB_REG_P so that it is included in the notification push channel.

Federating notifications to SAP Build Work Zone

If you are using SAP S/4HANA alongside other SAP Cloud solutions, you should consider providing a unified experience across your solutions by using the SAP Fiori launchpad in SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition. 

Important: Make sure your notifications are working in your local SAP Fiori launchpad of SAP S/4HANA before federating them to SAP Build Work Zone. 

You need to set up some configuration in SAP Build Work Zone and SAP S/4HANA. This includes activating the push channel for SAP_CLOUD - among other prerequisites as shown below. 

Important: Do not leave this configuration in an inconsistent state - as it can cause errors impacting running processes such as flexible workflow and situations. 

Refer to:

  • SAP Help - SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition – Administration – Managing Notifications
  • SAP Help - SAP Gateway - Notification Channel - Add Push for SAP Business Technology Platform

When sending notifications to the internet you should consider whether you want to apply Secure Store and Forward (SSF) & other additional protections for potentially sensitive data.  This requires:

  • SSF to be configured in SAP Build Work Zone
  • Sensitive Data Cache to be set in the ABAP Platform (SAP Gateway) of your SAP S/4HANA system
  • Notification Providers to provide sensitive texts (for internet notifications) versus non-sensitive texts (when in SAP S/4HANA directly) - see the examples in blog post Part 3 

Refer to

  • SAP Help - SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition – Administration - Enabling Notifications From SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP Help - SAP Gateway  - Notification Channel - Activate Sensitive Data Cache and Data Encryption
  • SAP Help - SAP Gateway - Notification Channel - Notification Channel Secure Data Store

Providing push notifications

If your users frequently use mobile devices for their work tasks, you may want to consider push notifications. 

Hint: Mobile devices includes tablets, smartphones, foldable phones, smartwatches, Apple Vision Pro, etc.

The recommended approach is to use the launchpad on SAP Mobile Start to provide push notifications on mobile devices. Both Apple and Android devices are supported. To use SAP Mobile Start you must first configure SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition.

Refer to:

  • SAP Help - SAP Mobile Start - Administration guide - Configuration - Setting up SAP Mobile Start - Notifications
  • SAP Help - SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition - Administration - SAP Mobile Start - Setting Up the SAP Mobile Start Application

Hint: The earlier use of SAP Mobile Platform (SMP) for push notifications is no longer recommended as SMP has been sunset. Refer to SAP Note 3338016 - What is the future of SAP Mobile Platform on-premise beyond 2023?

Echoing notifications to email

Some users may prefer to receive SAP Fiori notifications via email.  This is particularly common where there are a large amount of casual/infrequent users. 

There are 3 configurations to check, followed by advice on how to confirm which notifications are email-enabled at runtime.

  1. Per system, ABAP Push Channel SAP_EMAIL is active for sending emails
  2. Per notification type, the provider must have enabled echoing notifications to emails
  3. Per user, the user must have enabled echoing their notifications of the relevant notification type to emails

Per system – verify the ABAP Push Channel for email is activated 

Push channels are configured in transaction /IWNGW/VNPUSHP. For Email Notifications, enter the push channel id SAP_EMAIL and mark it as Is Active - as shown in the example below. 

Once the push channel is active, users will see the Email option in the SAP Fiori launchpad > User Actions > Settings > Notifications for relevant notifications.

Worth knowing: If you are using the SAP Fiori launchpad of SAP Build Work Zone, you can also provide echo of  notifications to email. Refer to SAP Help - SAP Build Work Zone - Admin - Managing Notifications - Configuring an SMTP Mail Destination

Per notification type – the notification provider must permit echo to email notification

  1. Choose the Notification Type for which you want to enable email Notification.
  2. In the Notification Provider class, set the attribute IS_EMAIL_ENBLD to X

Per User – the user opts to echo notifications to email 

Prerequisite: As a user, you must have your email address maintained in the User Master maintained by your administrator - i.e. in transaction SU01. As a business user you can confirm your email address in the SAP Fiori launchpad > User Actions > Settings > User Account as shown in the examples below.

 

To set your personal notification preferences, you must:

  1. Login to SAP Fiori launchpad
  2. Navigate to User Actions - Settings - Notifications.
  3. Enabled echo to email for each notification type by ticking the Email checkbox and then selecting Save.

Any notifications triggered after you the change - that are enabled for email - will send the email notification to the user’s email address.

Further information on how to configure Email Notifications can be found in this blog post Enable Email Notification in Notification Channel Framework

FAQ and troubleshooting tips

Questions covered:

  • As an administrator, how can I see which notification texts are generated for users?
  • As an administrator, how can I check which notifications are email enabled at runtime?
  • As an administrator, can I clear/delete notifications on behalf of a user?

 

As an administrator, how can I see which notification texts are generated for users?

For workflow notifications, you can review the assigned texts in transaction SWF_PUSH_NOTIF1

For situation notifications, you can review the assigned texts in SAP Fiori apps Manage Situation Types and Manage Situation Types – Extended.

For process-specific workflows, the easiest option is to trigger the notification and use ADT in Eclipse (or transaction SE16) to check the notification cache table /IWNGW/NTYPE_TT for the template text generated.

To confirm the source notification provider, you can map the value of Notification Type Id to the entry in table /IWNGW/NTYPE.

Hint: This is one of very few areas in SAP S/4HANA were there is no CDS View provided.

As an administrator, how can I check which notifications are email enabled at runtime?

As an administrator, you can use some simple checks to review the underlying runtime data to check what is happening.  

In table /IWNGW/NTYPE you can see whether email notifications are enabled for each combination of notification provider + notification type + notification type version.  For example, you can view the content in ADT in Eclipse (or transaction SE16).

As an administrator, can I find out who whether notifications have been seen or read by users? 

Yes, you can find these details by evaluating the CDS Database View /IWNGW/INCONSMPT 

As an administrator, can I clear/delete notifications on behalf of a user?

Yes, by running transaction /IWNGW/H_CLEAR_NOTIF

Becoming a SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA guru

You’ll find much more on our  SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA topic page

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA RIG and Customer Care team.

entry.title: Account Assignment Objects in Asset Master Data in Universal Parallel Accounting (UPA)
entry.content:
Purpose

In UPA, Asset Master Data supports various account assignment objects. They all appear in the time-dependent data (time-interval) of an asset master record. 

As Asset Accounting (AA) has a lot of integrations with other applications, the way that AA handles account assignment objects is very complex. In the past, even inconsistent data can be stored into database.

In order to avoid these situations, AA in UPA has some restrictions in account assignment objects to ensure the data consistency. This article describes the most important restrictions and their backgrounds.

Account Assignment Objects

Account Assignment Objects are used to collect costs or expenses. Cost center, profit center and segment are most widely used objects. In most cases, they are marked as 'Active' in the view V_FAA_ACCTASN, as shown in the screenshot below. And then, they can be used in the asset master records.

Profit Center and Segment are usually marked as 'Relevant to Balance Sheet' in addition. Because they are often used to show asset balance in profit center / segment reportings. As a result, they are mandatory fields in asset master data.

Time-Dependent Data of Asset Master Data

Account Assignment Objects appear in the section of time-dependent data (time-interval) in asset master record.

When you create a new asset master record, the profit center is derived from the cost center or WBS element, and then the segment from the profit center. Therefore, although they appear to be read-only by default, system ensures they must have values. This derivation also ensures the cost center and its profit center are always consistently matching.

After saving this master data as shown in the screenshot above, the cost center 101011752 and the profit center YB600 can be used to collect depreciation expense.

⚠️Hint: You can add multiple time-intervals at a time on creation. If some of the intervals contain exactly the same values, (e.g. same cost center, same profit center, same plant, and even same room, etc.), after saving the asset master, they will be merged into one single time-interval.

Restrictions in CO

Cost Center is a time-dependent CO object. The same cost center can belong to different profit centers over different periods of time.

In asset master data, they are time-dependent as well. To avoid confusing the end users, both AA and CO have restrictions in the object definition in UPA:

  • Once a cost center has multiple time-intervals with assignments to different profit centers, it cannot be used in asset master data anymore.
  • Vice versa. Once a cost center has been assigned to an asset master record, in CO the cost center cannot have one more time-interval with assignment to a different profit center.

Restrictions in WBS Element

An asset under construction (AuC) can be generated from a WBS element. In the definition of a WBS element, you can enter cost center and profit center too. Unfortunately, even today, you can still enter invalid combination of cost center and profit center in your WBS element.

For example, you have defined the following CO objects.

Cost CenterProfit Center
APC1
BPC2

It is obvious that cost center A and profit center PC2 should not work together. However, you can still enter this meaningless combination (cost center A and profit center PC2) in your WBS element.

When an AuC is created from this WBS element, AA recognizes this inconsistency. As the consequence, only the profit center PC2 is taken over to the master record of AuC, but without cost center being filled in the AuC. ➡️ This is because AA cannot tell which one is intended by the end user. Profit center is taken, simply because it is marked as 'Relevant to Balance Sheet' and might be 'more correct'.

You can go back to your WBS element, to correct your cost center and profit center. And they will be adjusted in the asset master record of the AuC.

⚠️Hint: If you are changing the cost center / project center in your WBS element, it can happen that the your changes do not reach the asset master record of the AuC. It is mostly likely that the combination of cost center and profit center is wrong. Please check it in your WBS element.

Restrictions in AA

You will have these restrictions especailly when your asset has documents posted.

First, you can only change an existing time-interval, if it is the only or last time-interval. The reason behind is simple. Imagine you have two time-intervals, first one with profit center PC1 and the second with PC2. That means asset balance has been transferred from PC1 to PC2 already. You should not go to the first time-interval to change the profit center and destroy the consistency.

Second, you are not always allowed to change the cost center in an existing time-interval. To make it simpler to explain, let's say it is the only time-interval in your master record. And there, you want to change your cost center from A to B.

If both cost centers ares assigned to the same profit center PC1 (like the following), this change is OK. That is because this asset always stays with profit center PC1. Asset balance for PC1 is not affected.

Cost CenterProfit Center
APC1
BPC1

On the contrary, if the two cost centers belong to different profit centers, as shown in the table below, system will reject the change with the error message FAA_MD122.

Cost CenterProfit Center
APC1
BPC2

This error message tells you why this change is not allowed. Imagine: if the system had allowed the change, what would happen? The asset master record would show you that it had always stayed with the profit center PC2 from the beginning. However, in the asset reporting, you would see asset balance used to stay with profit center PC1.

Summary

All these restrictions and validations are designed to prevent inconsistent data from sneaking into the database.

If you want to collect your costs into a different profit center, always add a new time-interval and change the profit center there. Then, after your saving master data, asset balance will be transferred to the new profit center with a transfer document.

⚠️ Hint: for more details about transfer asset balance from within asset master record, refer to the blog posting here: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/account-assignment-changes-and-follow-up-postings-in-universal-parallel/ba-p/14114182

entry.title: Account Assignment Change and Follow-Up Posting in Asset Master Data in UPA
entry.content:
Purpose

In the past, Account Assignment Change was known as Re-organization in Asset Master Data. It is used to transfer asset balance to a different profit center / segment. 

This article describes this feature in Universal Parallel Accounting (UPA), with a concrete example, focusing on the improvements since its earlier version.

⚠️Hint: For more information about the account assignment objects in time-dependent data of asset master record, please refer to this page: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/account-assignment-objects-in-asset-master-data-in-universal-parallel/ba-p/14114128 

Example

In this example,

  • The company code has 2 open fiscal years: 2024 & 2025.
  • The asset has an acquisition on Jan 1st of 2024, with amount 12,000 EUR. 
  • The depreciation amount in each period is 100 EUR.

This asset has two time-intervals.

  • The first one is assigned to the profit center YB600, up to Jan 31 of 2024.
  • Next, you want to add the 2nd time-interval starts from Feb 1st of 2024, with the profit center YB700.

Limitations in Early Days

In the early days, the so-called re-organization shall be executed in exactly the following steps. 

  • Post depreciation up to the period right before the re-organization.
    In this example, you must post depreciation in period 1 first. This is to ensure all asset transactions under YB600 are fully booked in FI documents.
  • Add the new time-interval with the re-organization.
    In this example, add the new time-interval starting from Feb 1st of 2024, with the profit center YB700.
    By doing this, the complete asset balance is transferred from YB600 to YB700.
  • Post depreciation in period 2
    Now, the depreciation will be posted to YB700.

Limitation 1: You cannot trigger the re-organization without posting depreciation in period 1. That is because asset transactions under YB600 haven't been fully booked yet.

Limitation 2: After the re-organization, you cannot post any document back in period 1. Otherwise, the balance in previous profit center YB600 cannot remain zero anymore. ➡️ And that is why you must post depreciation in period 1 beforehand.

Limitation 3: Re-organization can only happen in 1st open fiscal year. ➡️ Otherwise, you have to face the problems mentioned in 'Limitation 2'.

Account Assignment Change and Follow-up Posting Today

With the improvements in UPA, all limitations above have been removed.

You can make account assignment change without posting depreciation in period 1 beforehand.

That is because after account assignment change, the depreciation postings in period 1 can be automatically transferred to the YB700.

With that, the limitation 3 disappears automatically. You can make account assignment change in the upper open fiscal year too.

⚠️Hint: Your new time-interval with the account assignment change shall not cover any existing postings. In this example, if there is any posting existing on / after Feb 1st of 2024, you are not allowed to trigger this account assignment change. ➡️ The reason is clear: these existing documents have been posted to the previous profit center YB600. If you want to transfer all the balance from YB600, you need a new time-interval with a later starting date. 

Summary

The improvements in Account Assignment Change in UPA benefit customers a lot, with all limitation from early days removed.

One thing worth mentioning here is about how to cancel the account assignment change. That means you don't want to have any balance under YB700. Instead, you want to go back to YB600.

  • The first thing you must make sure that there is no document posted after the date of account assignment change, i.e. no new transactions for YB700. 
  • If there is any, reverse them.
  • Now, as the last step, delete the time-interval for this account assignment change.

⚠️Hint: To reversel normal asset postings, you can simply do it by clicking on the document number in 'Value Display' of Manage Fixed Assets app. And then, click on 'Reverse Journal Entries - In Asset Accounting' from the context menu. However, to revese an account assignment change, you must delete the corresponding time-interval for this account assignment change. This is the only way.

entry.title: Drive Process-centric SAP S/4HANA Transformation with SAP Signavio
entry.content:
When organizations move to SAP S/4HANA, it’s never “just” a system upgrade. It’s a fundamental transformation of how your business runs – from technology and data, to processes and people. To succeed, organizations need clarity, alignment, and speed across all phases of the journey. This is where SAP Signavio plays a critical role.

In this blog, we will explore how SAP Signavio supports each phase of your S/4HANA transformation and why Business Transformation Management (BTM) should be treated as a continuous capability rather than a one-off project. Whether you’re starting fresh (Greenfield) or converting an existing system (Brownfield), SAP Signavio helps you build on a foundation of transparency, collaboration, and continuous improvement.

 

Greenfield vs. Brownfield: Why It Matters

Not all SAP S/4HANA transformations are the same. Understanding the difference is key to tailoring your approach:

  • Greenfield means (re-)implementing SAP from scratch. You start fresh with new system configuration and process design. It offers maximum flexibility and transformation potential and eliminates legacy complexity.
  • Brownfield is a system conversion from an existing SAP ERP to S/4HANA preserving data and processes.

Greenfield and brownfield are often presented as opposites, and for simplicity, this blog distinguishes between their classic forms. In reality, they represent strategic trade-offs rather than a clear-cut “right” or “wrong” choice, with many organizations operating in the grey area between them. Greenfield enables full process redesign and innovation but demands greater change management and alignment. Brownfield accelerates the transition by preserving existing investments yet may also retain legacy inefficiencies. In practice, many companies adopt a selective or hybrid approach to align with their business priorities and risk tolerance. Because each path differs in scope and objectives, SAP Signavio supports them in tailored ways—particularly during the discovery, preparation, and design phases. While both approaches are applicable to S/4HANA Private Cloud, Greenfield is the default choice for S/4HANA Public Cloud, as its standardized and constrained environment aligns best with a fresh, best-practice-driven implementation.

 

Process-Centric SAP S/4HANA Transformation with SAP Signavio

Using SAP Signavio in an S/4HANA transformation gives organizations clear visibility into current processes and data, enabling them to address key value drivers. It promotes adherence to standardized SAP processes by identifying custom transactions, deviations from best practices, and performance gaps. Signavio also drives process harmonization by exposing redundant variants and tracking outliers. On the data side, it helps reduce management costs by highlighting unused configurations, master data, and transactional backlog. Additionally, it reveals automation opportunities and areas of manual rework, supporting more efficient, scalable operations.

 SAP Activate is SAP’s implementation methodology designed to guide organizations through the deployment of SAP solutions. It’s clear, phased approach – from Discover to Run – provides a practical lens to explore how SAP Signavio supports each step of an S/4HANA transformation. By aligning SAP Signavio’s capabilities with Activate phases, you can see where and how to leverage SAP Signavio throughout the entire journey towards continuous improvement.

 

1) Process Performance and Execution analysis

In the Discover stage of an ERP transformation, SAP Signavio's process performance and execution analysis capabilities play a crucial role in building a data-driven foundation for decision-making. This will help bridge the gap between gut feeling and reality. It brings fact-based clarity to ERP transformation planning, ensuring that you don't just implement a new system—you improve how your business runs.

The goal of the discover stage is to understand business value, assess transformation readiness, and define strategy. SAP Signavio supports transformation initiatives by providing transparency into value identification, process analysis, and opportunities for improvement and harmonization. It also enables the definition of measurable goals and supports the evaluation, usage, and clean-up of current data.

Especially relevant for Greenfield
  • Collect all your insights and reveal which processes and how they actually run in the source ERP system based on real transactional data
  • Identify inefficiencies and automation opportunities in key processes that impact performance, compliance, or costs – such as bottlenecks, rework, or delays. Example: Find delayed invoice due to manual corrections leading to an automation effort that cuts invoice cycle time.
  • Identify opportunities to harmonize process variants across regions or business units to reduce complexity. Example: Compare invoice approval processes across 8 countries and unify them into a single variant, improving transparency and compliance.
  • Benchmark your current process KPIs against industry standards to identify where you underperform, highlight practices worth scaling across your organization. Example: Discover that the touchless order rate is lagging, prompting process standardization across regions.
  • Define target process KPIs to track improvement over time and define transformation goals (e.g., reduce throughput time by X%).
  • Conduct root cause analysis to identify what drives inefficiencies or deviations – like delays, rework, or long cycle times. Example: Uncover that purchase order approvals are delayed due to missing documentation, which was fixed by introducing mandatory checklists.
  • Evaluate current processes to determine what to retire, reimagine, or automate as part of your new design.
  • Define transformation initiatives based on desired outcomes and quantify their potential value to support data-driven decisions over intuition.
Relevant for Both Greenfield and Brownfield

In this blog, we explore the extreme opposite scenarios, but in practice, several of the Greenfield activities could be relevant for both approaches.

  • Identify and clean inconsistent or outdated master, configuration and transactional data to avoid migrating legacy issues and reduce transformation risk. Example: Detect fragmented supplier records, which were cleansed before project kick-off, improving procurement reliability.
  • Detect and retire unused or obsolete transaction codes, simplifying your system before conversion. Example: Reduce the transaction code catalog pre-migration by identifying codes unused for over 12 months.
Especially Relevant in Brownfield

These actions help you make informed improvements within your current system constraints.

  • Pinpoint quick wins that can be implemented without disrupting existing operations – such as automation, harmonization, or policy changes.

 

2) Process Design, Collaboration and Governance

In the Prepare and Explore stages of an ERP transformation, SAP Signavio’s Process Design, Collaboration, and Governance capabilities serve as the bridge between understanding what needs to change and aligning on how to change it – across teams, geographies, and business units. These capabilities ensure that your transformation is not just technically sound, but also business-led and collaborative.

Prepare sets the foundation for a successful project launch, while Explore focuses on validating business requirements and defining the to-be process landscape. SAP Signavio translates insights from Discover into future-state designs, aligned with enterprise architecture and supported by strong process governance.

Relevant for Greenfield
  • Design a centralized governance model from the start, defining roles, responsibilities, and approval workflows to govern (target) process design and manage process ownership at scale. Example: Set up a process transformation governance to enable faster decision-making and accountability during the rollout of new global templates.
  • Model and validate your current processes using real performance data, linking business understanding with operational reality. Example: Map current invoice handling process and discover a performance drop in a specific region.
  • Align business processes and IT architecture by integrating process models with LeanIX avoiding misalignment between what the business needs and what IT delivers. Example: Avoid costly system gaps by linking its claims process design directly with enterprise architecture components.
  • Explore industry specific Reference Business Architecture models to define a target operating model in order to align stakeholders around standardized processes and scope accordingly. Example: Use SAP Signavio’s reference content to design a unified global sales process across four countries.
  • Design your target processes and standardize based on SAP best practices in fit-to-standard workshops, adjusting only where business value clearly justifies it. Example: Import standard billing processes, accelerating workshop preparation and reducing workshop time.
  • Analyze actual system usage to inform solution decisions, including where T-code-based activities can be replaced with modern Fiori apps. Example: Identify that a legacy transaction used by many users could be streamlined via a Fiori app, improving UX and reducing training costs.
  • Document required gaps and deviations and define their rationale clearly, so configuration and development efforts are tightly scoped. Example: Identify required gaps in the process and log each with annotations and ownership.

 

3) From Target Design to Implementation

In the Realize and Deploy stages of an ERP transformation, the focus is on configuring the system based on approved processes and preparing the organization for adoption. SAP Signavio’s Process Management and Governance capabilities – integrated with SAP Cloud ALM – help turn design into reality.

During Realize, SAP Signavio provides the validated to-be process design as a baseline for defining the solution design in SAP Cloud ALM. Centralized documentation and governance support process validation, traceability, and consistency throughout the implementation.

In Deploy, the emphasis shifts to go-live readiness and this is where training and adoption starts. SAP Signavio enables clear communication of process changes, supports training, and fosters alignment between business and IT. Post go-live, governance provides accountability and a strong foundation for continuous improvement.

Relevant for Greenfield
  • Define configuration blueprints based on approved to-be process models, syncing directly to SAP Cloud ALM or other tools to guide consultants and developers and create a shared language between business and IT. Example: Avoid costly configuration rework during testing or misinterpretation using BPMN models as a single source of truth for developers and consultants.
  • Generate training materials directly from your approved process models, streamlining onboarding content creation and accelerate user onboarding for new ways of working
  • Package process visuals and guidance by role, using Collaboration Hub to share only what’s relevant to each team. Example: Ensure warehouse, procurement, and finance teams each receive tailored documentation, leading to faster adoption and fewer errors.

 

4) Value realization, monitoring, and continuous improvement

In the Run phase of an ERP transformation, SAP Signavio’s capabilities help organizations monitor operations, uncover inefficiencies, and drive continuous improvement. This phase ensures that the transformation delivers sustained value – not just once, but over time.

The Run phase focuses on stable operations and ongoing optimization. With real-time data and process mining, SAP Signavio enables business and IT to detect performance gaps and compliance risks, and act quickly to improve efficiency and user experience. Like some of the activities in the Explore phase. With these insights, organizations can take targeted action to enhance process efficiency, realize value, improve user experience, and adapt to evolving business needs.

By aligning improvement efforts across teams and regions through governance and collaboration, SAP Signavio turns transformation into a lasting capability, supporting a culture of continuous improvement – where processes don’t just support the business, they drive it forward.

Relevant for Both Greenfield & Brownfield
  • Monitor live process performance to track whether your key targets are being met – like lead times, automation rates, or exception volumes. Example: Track delivery lead times post-go-live and identify a delay in one region, enabling immediate intervention.
  • Track conformance: check whether users are actually following the new processes or reverting to legacy workarounds. Example: Discover that 38% process variants in a specific country were still done using a variant not being part of the baseline. After a targeted enablement push, Fiori app adoption rose.
  • Identify process bottlenecks or cost drivers and launch data-driven improvement initiatives using drill-down analysis.
  • Continuously track transformation goals and assess how effectively value is being realized by monitoring process performance, identifying deviations, and aligning improvement actions with expected business outcomes.
  • Track system usage patterns over time – including adoption of new tools and apps – to guide training, support, and change management. Example: Identify underused Fiori apps and prioritize those for follow-up training during the quarterly enablement cycle.
  • Track data over time – identify and clean inconsistent or outdated master, configuration and transactional data to avoid migrating legacy issues and reduce transformation risk.
  • Track transaction code usage over time – detect and retire unused or obsolete transaction codes, creating understanding of how the system is used and how processes are adopted.

For Brownfield, many of the activities done during a Greenfield transformation can be applied post go-live. Such as target process design, value identification, process harmonization etc.

 

Transformation Is Ongoing: Treat it as a Capability

Business Transformation Management (BTM) is essential for organizations undergoing complex change, such as an SAP S/4HANA transformation. It ensures that strategic goals are effectively translated into operational reality through structured governance, cross-functional alignment, and measurable outcomes. Without BTM, transformation efforts risk becoming disjointed, short-lived, or misaligned with business goals.

SAP Signavio plays a key role and is more than just a tool for managing a transformation project – it is a strategic enabler for ongoing transformation. A successful SAP S/4HANA go-live is not the finish line, but the start of a new way of working. To sustain value, organizations must embrace Business Transformation Management (BTM), and Business Process Management (BPM)as long-term capabilities embedded into everyday operations.

By embedding BPM into the DNA of daily operations, organizations gain the ability to continuously align strategy with execution, monitor real-world performance, and foster a culture of data-driven, collaborative improvement. At the same time, BTM provides a structured approach to govern change, manage complexity, and ensure cross-functional alignment throughout the transformation journey.

SAP Signavio empowers businesses to embed these capabilities into their core operations – keeping processes up-to-date, performance-driven, and flexible. This foundation enables organizations to navigate change confidently, foster ongoing innovation, and maximize the long-term value of their transformation and future initiatives.

 

#S/4HANAtransformation #Greenfield #Brownfield #BusinessTransformationManagement #BTM #SAPSignavio #ERPTransformation #integratedToolchain #SAPTransformation #BusinessTransformation #SAPActivate

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Build: Highlights from SAP Sapphire 2025
entry.content:
As the SAP Sapphire 2025 global keynote outlined, this year's focus was clear: AI, applications, and data. SAP Build continues to evolve in alignment with these themes, helping organizations efficiently build, automate, and extend their business processes and applications.

Here's a roundup of Sapphire's key SAP Build announcements and innovations to watch this year.

Joule Studio: Intelligent AI Agents and Skills Builder

Joule Studio Capabilities

SAP unveiled Joule Studio, a new environment within SAP Build that helps you create, deploy, and manage AI agents and custom skills. With Joule Studio, teams can enrich Joule with company-specific skills and intelligent agents that work across SAP and third-party systems. Joule Studio brings two powerful capabilities:

  • Joule Skills: Design modular, reusable skills that can be invoked during conversations. These skills can trigger actions, pull data, and orchestrate SAP Build components like workflows, automations, or apps.
  • AI Agents: Go beyond skills by building full-fledged AI agents that dynamically execute business tasks using reasoning capabilities. These agents use business context, data from SAP systems, and large language models to adapt to user needs.

Joule Studio will be available by the end of Q2 2025, with Agent Builder capabilities expected in Q4 2025.

Early adopters like Sovanta, an SAP partner, already use Joule Studio to enhance customer service. Sovanta significantly streamlined support processes and accelerated ticket resolution by integrating conversational skills with SAP systems.

Another early adopter, Pricefx - a global leader in cloud-based pricing software - is using Joule Studio to bring AI-driven intelligence into their solution. By developing an Agentic AI copilot, they aim to deliver contextual, business-aware guidance that enhances pricing decisions for their customers.

Interested in learning more? Register for the webcast: Meet Joule Studio: Drive Innovation with AI Agents & Joule Skills.

New AI Capabilities for Business Users and Developers

  • SAP Build Work Zone + Joule: Employees can now use Joule to find relevant apps, tasks, and documents faster. It can summarize or generate content based on your business data, saving time and boosting productivity.

Joule in SAP Build Work Zone

  • AI-Enhanced ABAP Development: Developers migrating to SAP Cloud ERP can use AI to convert custom ECC code into ABAP Cloud standards. This feature simplifies modernization efforts while ensuring code quality and compliance.

These capabilities are expected in Q3 2025.

 Expanded SAP Build Capabilities

SAP Build continues to grow as a unified platform with:

  • Extensibility Wizard for SAP Business Suite: A guided wizard that helps you build side-by-side extensions with the proper context, faster. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP SuccessFactors support is planned for release in H2 of 2025.

Extensibility Wizard in SAP Business Suite

Watch Sapphire Customer Session: Extending SAP S/4HANA Cloud with SAP Build at OGE: Open Grid Europe used SAP Build to extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud—boosting onboarding efficiency by 50% and reducing invoicing time by 20%.

Watch Sapphire Solution Demo: Extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud: SAP Build application development and automation. Discover how to rapidly personalize and extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud while maintaining a clean core.

  • SAP Business Data Cloud Integration: Planned for Q4 2025, SAP Build will offer deep integration with SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) via CAP. Developers can consume and expose data products using virtual tables in SAP HANA Cloud, accelerating the development of insight-rich apps while reducing manual integration work. This improves data consistency and supports scalable, compliant development.
  • Event-Driven Applications: SAP Build, in combination with SAP Integration Suite and CAP, will support event-based interactions across systems. General availability is expected in H2 2025.

Simplified Pricing and Packaging

  • SAP Build offers a unified pricing model that combines app development, automation, and workspaces under a single entitlement. This simplifies adoption, reduces licensing complexity, and allows teams to build and extend at their own pace. Learn more in this blog post.

Thanks for reading! Which SAP Build innovation are you most excited about? Let us know - and don't miss these key resources from SAP Sapphire 2025:

  • Watch Sapphire Global Keynote: Your Best. Made Real. No Matter What Lies Ahead
  • Read SAP Sapphire 2025 Innovation Guide
  • Visit the Session Catalog with On-Demand Sessions

entry.title: Coming Soon: Release Navigator for SAP Cloud ERP Private (former SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition)
entry.content:
Reducing the Intimidation of ERP Releases

It is widely acknowledged that software releases can be intimidating and overwhelming, particularly for those who lack adequate preparation. In times when releases seem daunting, the initial effort may feel more like a struggle to keep up. Other times, it often appears more convenient to inform your team, “We can simply wait for the next release and plan more effectively.” This scenario is commonplace, leading to a reactive approach to releases rather than a proactive one. Being reactive can create the sensation of merely striving to keep one’s “head above water” in an effort to maintain operational technology. To confront this issue, SAP Enterprise Support is unveiling a new tool designed to streamline access to the latest release content curated by SAP experts. This tool assembles and organizes information into a user-friendly digital library.

Introducing the Release Navigator: a resource aimed at helping customers derive maximum business value from SAP Cloud ERP Private by centralizing release information and content.  We encourage you to explore this valuable resource to enhance your approach to managing technology upgrades. 

The Release Navigator will be available later this month on the Release Ready page which you can access here:

 

 

 

Saving Time & Effort

The Release Navigator helps users and administrators understand new features that are coming with the release. It also assists with:

  1. Guidance on Releases: It offers guidance on important dates and new functionalities are included in the latest release, helping businesses to better prepare for updates.
  2. Support Materials: Provides access to various support materials such as release blogs, product documentation, guided demos, and videos that assist in leveraging new features effectively (refer to Figure 1: Product Specific page).
  3. Change Management: Helps with change management by ensuring that all stakeholders are informed about new features and can plan training or process adjustments accordingly.

Figure 1: Product Specific page

 

At launch, the following topics will be organized by the following:

Lines of Business (LoB)
  • Asset Management
  • Finance
  • Human Resources
  • Manufacturing
  • R&D/Engineering
  • Sales
  • Service
  • Sourcing & Procurement
  • Supply Chain
Industries
  • Automotive
  • Banking
  • Defense & Security
  • Insurance
  • Oil & Gas
  • Public Sector
  • Retail & Fashion
  • Utilities
Enterprise Technology
  • SAP Fiori
Artificial Intelligence (AI)
  • SAP Joule

Additional release content and information pertaining to SAP Cloud ERP Private that is not specific to a particular line of business (LoB), industry, enterprise technology, or artificial intelligence can be found on the General page (refer to Figure 2: General page). This section also provides links to other Release Navigators related to various SAP products and services, including Business Technology Platform and Supply Chain Management.

Figure 2: General page

 

Innovation Plan

To minimize disruptions to your user experience, the Release Navigator for SAP Cloud ERP Private incorporates new UX elements once a year, during the October update cycle, aligning with the introduction of new release content. The updates to the Release Navigator are fully automated, requiring no manual intervention.

 
For comprehensive details on product release schedules, please consult the Release Calendar card. This resource includes plans for UX innovation and content updates through 2025.
 
Find more details below:
 

 

Connect with Us

We value your input and encourage you to share any feedback or specific content requests for future updates to the Release Navigator. Please feel free to complete our 2-question anonymous survey or contribute your suggestions in the Comments section below this post.

You can also leave your feedback directly in the Release Navigator by selecting the User Feedback inside the side menu (refer to Figure 3: User Feedback page).

 

Figure 3: User Feedback page

Best Run,
Andrew Ford

entry.title: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Status Code Field Update
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

The SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, inbound and outbound invoicing options for Brazil team has updated the Status Code (cStat) field, which now supports 5 digits. The supported electronic fiscal documents can process and interpret the new rejection codes returned by SEFAZ.

You can implement these changes by following the instructions on SAP Notes 3605421 and 3601578.

Take a look at the latest updates and features in our What's New session on the SAP Help Portal:

  • SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP ERP

For a step-by-step guide on how to use the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, inbound and outbound invoicing options for Brazil, refer to the following SAP Help Portal pages:

  • Inbound Nota Fiscal
  • Outbound Nota Fiscal

You can sign up for email alerts when new SAP Notes are released or updated in the components listed below; just follow the instructions in SAP Knowledge Base Article 2171560.

  • CA-GTF-CSC-EDO-BR (Inbound invoicing option for Brazil)
  • LOD-LH-NFE (Outbound invoicing option for Brazil)

Use the regulatory change manager tool to determine whether a legal change has a planned solution. Take a look at SAP Knowledge Base Article 3421995 and the regulatory change manager page to understand how to use this tool.

Thank you, and until next time,
Thiago
